data.description:
**K&K Global Talent Solutions Inc.**
is an international recruiting agency that has been providing technical resources in the Canada and the USA region since 1993.

This position is with one of our clients in USA, who is actively hiring candidates to expand their teams.

**Job Title: Network Security Engineer (Zscaler ,Infoblox, F5 BIG-IP LTM/GTM Exp.)**

**Location:**
**Plano, TX /Onsite**

**Position type: Full time/Permanent role**

**Descriptions:**

**Must Have Technical/Functional Skills**

• Experience: 10 years in network security engineering, application delivery, or similar roles.

• Infoblox: Hands-on experience with Infoblox DDI/IPAM administration.

• Zscaler: Deep knowledge of Zscaler ZIA, ZPA, PAC files, and DLP engines.

• F5: Proficiency in F5 BIG-IP LTM/GTM administration, including iRules and SSL certificate management.

• Firewalling: Strong experience with Palo Alto Networks firewalls (Palo Alto experience preferred).

• Networking Fundamentals: In-depth knowledge of TCP/IP, BGP, OSPF, VLANs, and DNS.

• Automation: Familiarity with scripting languages (Python/Ansible) to streamline operations.

**Core Competencies**

• Troubleshooting: Expert analytical skills for debugging complex traffic, authentication, and network issues using Wireshark.

• Collaboration: Ability to work with cross-functional teams to align network security with business objectives.

• Communication: Strong written and verbal skills for creating procedures and reporting to stakeholders.

**Education & Certifications**

• Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or equivalent experience.

**Roles & Responsibilities**

• F5 Load Balancing & Application Delivery: Design, configure, and troubleshoot F5 BIG-IP LTM (Local Traffic Manager) and GTM (Global Traffic Manager) to ensure high availability, security, and performance of critical applications.

• Zscaler Security Suite (SASE): Implement and manage Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA) and Zscaler Private Access (ZPA) to secure user access and enforce policies for web traffic and private applications.

• Infoblox DDI Management: Deploy and manage Infoblox DNS, DHCP, and IP Address Management (IPAM) solutions. Responsible for core network functionality.

• Firewall & Security Policy: Configure and manage enterprise firewalls (e.g., Palo Alto Networks) to enforce network security policies, including NAT, zone-based firewalling, and threat prevention.

• Operational Excellence: Administer PAC files for proxy traffic routing, maintain SSL inspection policies, and troubleshoot network traffic flows.

• Documentation & Compliance: Create and maintain network documentation, including diagrams, configurations, and change management procedures, ensuring audit readiness.

**About Logicbroker, Inc.**
Logicbroker is the Agentic Commerce Orchestration Engine helping enterprise retailers, brands, suppliers, and distributors connect and grow. Our Intelligent Commerce Network powers over $10B in GMV for global leaders like Samsung, Walgreens, and Home Depot by automating the entire process from discovery to doorstep and stock to dock. We make products discoverable, shoppable, fulfillable, and returnable so our clients can grow faster, delight customers, and run smarter operations.
  
  
**Job Summary**
We are hiring a Growth Engineer in the marketing team — an AI-native builder who will leverage advanced tools to efficiently orchestrate marketing operations, integrated campaigns, and sales coordination. You’ll report to the VP, Marketing and work closely with our sales, events, content, and design team members. It’s never been a more exciting time for marketers - AI tools are giving the function enormous leverage and scale. As part of the Logicbroker marketing team, this role will harness the power of truly integrated AI capabilities to gain scale and speed across several focus areas, including (but not limited to):
  
  

- orchestrating advanced marketing operations workflows that accelerate our team
- diving deep into reporting to generate insights that drive growth
- architecting systems, workflows, and capabilities that amplify marketing’s successes
- owning key agency, contractor, and stakeholder relationships

The right candidate is an AI-native marketer rooted in fundamentals — good storytelling and a solid grasp on data and analytics. We’re not looking for someone who has all the answers, but for someone who knows how to leverage AI to curiously build systems, skills, workflows, and outcomes. This role is an accelerator to unlock higher levels of speed to market, scale, insight gathering, and cross-functional collaboration. Coding experience not required.
  
  
**What You'll Do**

- Scale AI-powered capabilities across the team


- Work through a project list of outcomes that evolve as we continue to scale – and deliver capabilities, workflows, and reports.
- Collaborate with Marketing, Sales, RevOps, Product, and other functions to gather requirements and enable teams on the capabilities you’ll build
- Focus on connecting key areas of the GTM motion to orchestrate faster and more precise go-to-market outcomes
- Create documentation and resources to help the team build new skills and agents at scale

- Drive Demand Generation programs


- Leverage AI to work with contractors, agencies, and internal resources to grow results
- Combine disparate reporting signals into a singular view
- Automate signals and workflows within our go-to-market motions

- Own Marketing reporting and insight


- Work with RevOps and AI tools to provide consistent, accurate reporting on Marketing KPIs
- Mine data for insights and escalate findings that can result in improved GTM performance

**What We Need**

- 3-5 years in B2B SaaS marketing – with experience in marketing automation
- Revenue Cycle and reporting familiarity
- Claude skills, projects, and agent builds for Marketing
- HubSpot or Salesforce CRM experience
- Strong communication, collaboration, and reporting skills

**Strong Plus**

- Agency experience or management of an agency
- Experience with optimization on major ad platforms (Google Ads, LinkedIn)
- Startup or scaling business experience oriented at Enterprise customers
- Ecommerce industry knowledge
- Board-level reporting outputs

**Why Logicbroker**
****Mission-Driven Culture:****
Be part of a company transforming digital commerce through innovation and agility—your work directly shapes how global brands connect with customers.
  
  
****Collaborative, No-Ego Environment:****
We believe the best ideas win, not the loudest voices. You’ll work alongside teammates who challenge and support each other.
  
  
****Hybrid Flexibility with High-Performance Energy:****
Whether remote or in-office, we foster autonomy and accountability—because we trust you to own your success.
  
  
****Leadership That Listens:****
Our executives are not just accessible—they’re invested in your growth, open to your ideas, and committed to building a company where people thrive.
  
  
****Celebrated Wins, Shared Learnings:****
From team offsites to Slack shoutouts, we celebrate progress and learn from setbacks together

**About Clearly AI**
Clearly AI automates the most painful bottleneck in the enterprise: security and privacy reviews. We help security teams complete high-quality threat models, privacy impact assessments, and vendor risk evaluations in minutes instead of weeks.
  
  
We're live with Fortune 500s and global brands including Rivian, Ericsson, HID Global, Affirm, Webflow, and Okta. We've reviewed thousands of security-critical products, from badge scanners to stablecoin platforms to autonomous driving features.
  
  
Backed by Y Combinator, Basis Set Ventures, Crosspoint Capital, Argon Ventures, and Ritual Capital. Selected as a Top 10 Finalist for the RSAC 2026 Innovation Sandbox Contest.
  
  
**Location**
Seattle/SF preferred, remote OK. We meet in person 1-2x per quarter. Applicants must be authorized to work in the United States without the need for visa sponsorship now or in the future.
  
  
**Why This Role Exists**
We've proven we can land and expand inside some of the world's most demanding security organizations. Now we need to build the machine that scales it.
  
  
This is not a RevOps generalist role or a traditional marketing ops hire. We're looking for someone who thinks like an engineer and operates like a strategist - someone who can design and build an AI-native GTM engine from scratch, then draw the insights that tell us what's actually working.
  
  
The right person has built real systems, written real code, and has strong opinions about how AI changes what's possible in go-to-market. You'll be the first person in this function, with a direct line to founders and a greenfield mandate.
  
  
**What You'll Own**

- GTM Stack Architecture & Build
- Design and implement our full GTM tech stack from the ground up - CRM, marketing and sales automation, data enrichment, and signal capture
- Evaluate, select, and integrate best-in-class tools for our motion (enterprise, security-focused, low-volume/high-ACV)
- Build for extensibility: every system should connect cleanly to the next
- Own the data model and ensure clean, consistent data flows across the stack
- AI-Native GTM Automation
- Design and deploy AI-powered workflows that automate research, outreach personalization, lead scoring, and pipeline hygiene
- Build agents and automations that let our small GTM team punch far above its weight
- Stay current on the rapidly evolving AI tooling landscape and make smart adoption decisions
- Avoid bloat: build systems that are powerful but maintainable
- Revenue Intelligence & Insight Generation
- Instrument our full funnel intelligence. From first touch through expansion - to surface what's actually driving revenue
- Build and evolve dashboards and reporting frameworks that let leadership make fast, informed decisions
- Identify patterns in win/loss data, pipeline velocity, and product usage that inform GTM strategy
- Partner with GTM leadership to translate data into clear recommendations, not just charts
- Demand Generation Infrastructure
- Build and maintain the systems that support outbound and inbound at scale
- Implement intent data, enrichment, and account scoring that helps us find and prioritize the right buyers
- Ensure our outreach infrastructure (deliverability, sequencing, personalization) is built to enterprise standards
- Support campaign execution with the tooling and automation it needs to work
- Website & Conversion Infrastructure
- Own the GTM layer of our website - CMS enhancement, chat and conversion tooling, form routing, and visitor intelligence
- Deploy and configure chat and lead capture tooling (e.g., Qualified, Drift, or similar) connected to CRM and routing logic
- Instrument the site for intent signals - who's visiting, what they're reading, and how that maps to pipeline
- Ensure website systems and related data connect cleanly to the broader GTM stack
- Cross-Functional Collaboration
- Work directly with GTM teams and leadership to understand what's slowing deals down and build tools that fix it
- Partner with the Technical Implementation and Engineering teams to connect product usage signals back into GTM motions
- Feed structured insights to product and engineering on what customer and prospect behavior is telling us

**What This Role Is NOT**

- Not a pure analyst or BI developer
- Not a tool administrator managing someone else's architecture decisions
- Not a demand gen manager focused on campaign execution
- Not a team builder - this is an individual contributor role with high ownership

**What We're Looking For**
**Skills:**
Revenue Operations, AI/ML, GTM Engineering, CRM Architecture, Data Analytics
  
  
**Required**

- 5+ years in GTM engineering, revenue operations, or a closely adjacent business intelligence technical function
- Hands-on experience building and/or owning a GTM tech stack - not just using it
- Strong working knowledge of AI/ML tools, APIs, and automation frameworks as applied to GTM workflows
- Experience building AI agents or LLM-powered automations for sales or marketing workflows
- Ability to read and understand code or scripts (Python, SQL, or similar) to build automations and data pipelines - not just configure no-code tools
- Experience with CRM architecture, data modeling, and pipeline instrumentation
- Strong analytical instincts: you know the difference between a vanity metric and a signal
- Comfortable operating with significant autonomy and setting/communicating priorities in an early-stage environment

**Strongly Preferred**

- Prior experience in a "GTM engineer" or "RevOps" role at a startup
- Familiarity with enterprise sales motions and long, complex deal cycles
- Experience with B2B cybersecurity or adjacent technical domains
- Strong opinions on tool selection - and the scars to back them up

**How Success Is Measured (First 12 Months)**

- GTM stack fully deployed and adopted, with clean data across all systems
- AI automations measurably reducing manual research and outreach time for AEs
- Revenue dashboard actively informing weekly GTM decisions
- Intent + enrichment model driving prioritized, high-quality pipeline
- Insights from funnel data visibly influencing product and GTM strategy

**Why Join Clearly AI**
Work directly with founders to build a GTM engine that didn't exist before you arrived. See your systems translate into pipeline, deals, and product direction - with full visibility into how the business grows.
  
  
We're an RSAC 2026 Innovation Sandbox Top 10 Finalist, backed by $8.4M in seed funding, with a real product and real customers in some of the most demanding security environments on the planet.
  
  
Competitive salary, equity, and benefits. Direct path to GTM leadership as the company scales.
  
  
For this role, the salary range is $150,000 - $210,000. These ranges are a guideline; actual base salary may vary based on various factors, including market and individual qualifications objectively assessed during the interview process, and the ranges for this role may be modified.
  
  
In addition, your compensation package may include additional components such as equity and benefits. Benefits may vary based on role, country, and local regulations.

**About us**

We are building Token Router, a developer-focused platform that helps users access, manage, and switch across multiple AI model APIs more easily. Our goal is to provide developers, AI startups, and product teams with a more flexible and efficient way to work with leading AI models across providers.

We are at an exciting early stage of product and growth, and we are looking for someone who is highly passionate about AI, execution-oriented, and eager to help us build a strong developer community, drive user growth, and improve the full journey from awareness to activation to retention.

**Role Mission**

We're hiring a Growth Engineer to own the full surface area of how PaleBlueDot AI's TokenRouter( tokenrouter.com) shows up to our two core audiences: startups/developers building on AI and enterprises adopting it. This is a client-facing, product-marketing-adjacent role spanning Discord community, email and newsletter campaigns, blog and social content (X, LinkedIn, Reddit), offline presence (meetups, workshops, hackathons), partnership outreach, brand development, developer relations, marketing campaigns, and incentive programs (referrals, credits, power-user rewards). We need someone who can run a community, ship a hackathon, close a partnership conversation, design a campaign that actually converts, and translate what startups and enterprise customers want back into product decisions, often in the same week.

**Key Responsibilities**

- The ideal hire is sharp enough to speak credibly about TokenRouter and the underlying model landscape, understanding what startups and enterprises actually need around usage management, evals, integration, cost control, and reliability, and can move fluently between both segments, recognizing that a scrappy seed-stage team and a Fortune 500 buyer have very different priorities, timelines, and channels. You're commercially minded enough to drive adoption and partnerships across both, and operationally strong enough to run multiple channels. **About Sift**
Sift is the data infrastructure platform for hardware engineering teams. Sift turns high-frequency telemetry into engineering insights for mission-critical systems. Teams use Sift to build and operate rockets, satellites, autonomous vehicles, energy systems, defense platforms, and more.
  
  
Founded by former SpaceX engineers who built the tools behind Dragon and Starlink, Sift is building the data infrastructure to herald the AI era for physical systems.
  
  
**About The Role**
Sift is hiring a builder at the intersection of AI, automation, and go-to-market execution. Our platform is trusted by the most demanding engineering teams in aerospace, defense, energy storage, and robotics. The people buying and using Sift move fast, operate in high-stakes environments, and expect the tools around them to keep up.
  
  
As our GTM motion scales, we need someone who can close the gap between our best ideas and what is actually running in production. Someone who can take a whiteboard full of AI use cases, help prioritize, build a plan, and ship them. Someone who treats the marketing and sales stack the same way an engineer treats a technical system: with rigor, curiosity, and a bias toward outcomes.
  
  
This is not a strategy role. It is a building role. You will build the AI and automation infrastructure that powers how Sift finds, engages, and converts customers. From outreach signal pipelines and competitive intelligence systems to sales enablement tools and inbound automation, you will architect and build the systems that make the GTM team faster and sharper without adding headcount. You will be the technical backbone of a marketing team that has already shipped AI in production and is ready to push further.
  
  
You will partner closely with marketing, ops, sales, and leadership to turn ideas into working systems. You will bring structure to ambiguity, ship things that work, and improve them when they do not.
  
  
**In This Role, You’ll**

- Architect and Build AI-Powered GTM Systems: Design and ship automation pipelines across outreach, competitive intel, sales enablement, inbound, events, content, and reporting. Own the AI infrastructure and code that makes the GTM team faster and more precise.
- Write and Maintain Production Code: Build and own Python scripts, APIs, and integrations that connect data sources, AI models, and GTM tools into reliable workflows. Push and maintain work in GitHub. Document what you build so others can understand and extend it. This includes building and maintaining the vector database infrastructure that powers Sift's GTM AI Operating System, from ingestion pipelines to embedding models to retrieval logic.
- Lead AI Workflow Development: Translate ideas from the team into working systems. Know when to use an AI model, when to use a rule, and when to use nothing at all. Build systems where AI output is reliable enough to trust in production.
- Build and Own the Knowledge Infrastructure: Design and maintain the vector database that stores Sift's institutional knowledge as embeddings for GTM. Own the ingestion pipelines that pull from various data sources. Define chunking strategies, embedding models, and retrieval logic that make the system accurate and fast.
- Contribute to the GTM Tech Stack: Collaborate with Ops to manage integrations across Slack, HubSpot, Clay, and other marketing tools in our stack. Keep systems clean, documented, and scalable as the business grows.
- Collaborate Across Marketing and Sales: Work directly with the marketing team and BDRs to understand workflow bottlenecks, identify automation opportunities, and ship solutions that actually get used.
- Drive the AI Roadmap: Help prioritize and sequence what to build next. Maintain a clear view of what is in production, what is in progress, and what is next. Surface tradeoffs and bring recommendations to leadership.
- Maintain and Improve What You Ship: Build systems designed to last. Monitor pipeline performance, catch failures before the team does, and improve workflows as needs change or data quality shifts.

**The Skillset You’ll Bring**

- You write Python: You know Python well enough to build, debug, and own your own scripts and pipelines without help. You are comfortable with APIs, data manipulation, and working in a GitHub repo. You can read someone else's code and improve it.
- You have worked with vector databases: You understand how embedding-based retrieval works and have built or maintained a RAG pipeline in production. You know how to chunk documents, choose an embedding model, evaluate retrieval quality, and debug when the wrong context comes back. Experience with Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, or similar is expected.
- You have shipped real automation: Not configured a Zapier template. Built a system with inputs, outputs, failure handling, and real users depending on it. You can walk through exactly what you built, what broke, and how you fixed it.
- You build with AI in production: You understand prompt engineering, API integration, and output validation. You know what AI is good at and where it fails. You have built workflows where the AI output feeds into something real, and you know how to make that reliable.
- You know the GTM context: You understand what a BDR needs to find a good lead, what slows down a sales cycle, and what makes a piece of enablement actually useful in a deal. You have worked near a marketing or revenue team before and do not need the basics explained.
- You are self-sufficient: You can go from a vague idea to a working prototype without a spec document or a dedicated engineering team. You ask the right questions, fill in the gaps, and ship.
- You work well with non-technical teammates: You can explain what you are building and why it matters without making people feel lost. You listen before you build and check in before you assume.
- You are curious and current: You follow the AI tooling space closely, bring new ideas to the team, and have opinions about what is worth using and what is hype.

**Location & Travel**
Sift’s headquarters is in
**Marina Del Rey, CA**
(Next to LAX). We collaborate
**in person**
twice a week—on
**Mondays and Thursdays**
—and come together for a full week every two months. We are open to
**relocating candidates to LA**
or
**working from our San Francisco**
office for the right candidate.
  
  
Salary range: $140,000 - $180,000 per year. Plus bonus, equity, and benefits.
  
  
**Eligibility**
**US Person Required**
: Must be a
**U.S. Citizen or Green Card Holder**
due to ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) / EAR (Export Administration Regulations) compliance requirements.

**TL;DR:**
We're hiring a Lead GTM Engineer to work directly with our Head of Growth to scale Listen from $XX → $XXX ARR. You're a technical, systems-oriented, and energetic operator who's excited to join a fast-growing, early-stage startup in a career-defining role. You're willing to roll up your sleeves – no task is too small when it helps move the company forward.
  
  
Min. 5+ years in GTM operations, sales engineering, or RevOps, ideally with experience building outbound motions and SDR systems. Prior experience in technical sales, data engineering, or growth/marketing operations is a plus.
  
  
**Background**
Listen Labs is an AI-powered research platform that helps teams uncover insights from customer interviews in hours — not months. We help customers analyze conversations, surface themes, and make faster, smarter product decisions.
  
  
**Company highlights — entirely product-led:**

- World-Class Team: Founded by serial entrepreneurs (previous AI exit), former co-founders, and talent from Jane Street, Twitter, Stripe, Affirm, Bain, Goldman Sachs, and many more Sequoia-backed startups (plus IOI/ICPC backgrounds).
- Hypergrowth: We’re a 40-person team backed by Sequoia, growing from $0 to a $14M run-rate in under a year. We move fast, care deeply about craft, and love working with people who take ownership.
- Traction: Rapid growth across segments with enterprise wins at Google, Microsoft, Nestlé, and P&G.
- Performance: Industry-leading win rate driven by a highly differentiated product.
- Market Validation: Consistently winning customers across all segments with over six-figure lands that lead to quick expansions.
- Viral Product: Interviews are shared with tens of thousands of viewers, fueling PLG, organic expansion, and daily inbound from Fortune 500s.

**What You’ll Do**

- Outbound infrastructure: Design and build scalable systems for prospecting, enrichment, and outreach – owning the technical backbone of our outbound motion from data pipelines to automation workflows.
- SDR enablement & optimization: Equip SDRs with world-class tooling, playbooks, and data insights to maximize efficiency and conversion rates across the funnel.
- Data & targeting strategy: Build ICP models, scoring frameworks, and segmentation logic to ensure outbound efforts are laser-focused on high-intent, high-fit accounts.
- Tech stack ownership: Evaluate, implement, and optimize GTM tools (CRM, sequencing, enrichment, intent data) to create a seamless, high-performance outbound engine.
- Performance analytics: Track, analyze, and report on outbound metrics – from reply rates to pipeline contribution – and drive continuous improvement through experimentation and iteration.
- Cross-functional alignment: Work with Heads of Growth, Sales, Product, and Engineering to ensure outbound systems integrate seamlessly with broader GTM strategy and product capabilities.

**Who You Are**

- You're a tooling expert, opinionated about best-in-class GTM tech stacks and experienced in evaluating, implementing, and optimizing tools that drive outbound performance at scale.
- You're hands-on and resourceful, comfortable shifting between high-level strategy and execution, from designing data models to writing SQL queries or automation scripts yourself.
- You're highly analytical and creative, able to blend technical problem-solving with strategic thinking about go-to-market efficiency and scale.
- You care deeply about craft and impact, not just output. You take pride in doing great work, even on small details.
- You're curious and collaborative, eager to learn from teammates across functions and energized by talking to SDRs and sales reps to understand their workflows and build systems that drive results.

**Life at Listen Labs**

- Competitive Compensation: We’re backed by world-class investors, including Sequoia Capital, Conviction, AI Grant, and Pear VC, and offer competitive compensation packages with meaningful equity ownership.

- Over $30B in market cap has been created in adjacent industries (Medallia, AlphaSense, GLG, Ipsos, Kantar). Our Sequoia partner, Bryan Schreier, was the first investor in Qualtrics – a $12B company tackling similar problems to ours.

- Benefits that Support You: Comprehensive healthcare and dental coverage, flexible time off to recharge, and an environment that values balance and trust.
- Room to Grow: As an early member of the team, you’ll have the opportunity to have end-to-end ownership over new responsibilities, shape processes from scratch, and grow alongside the company. We value people who want to stretch beyond their role and build something lasting.

**About Faire**
Faire is a technology wholesale platform built on the belief that the future is local. Independent retailers around the globe collectively represent a multi-hundred-billion-dollar wholesale market that has historically been fragmented and offline. At Faire, we're using the power of tech, data, and machine learning to connect this thriving community of entrepreneurs across the globe. Picture your favorite boutique in town — we help them discover the best products from around the world to sell in their stores. With the right tools and insights, we believe that we can level the playing field so businesses can grow and local communities can thrive.
  
  
We’re looking for smart, resourceful and passionate people to join us as we power the shop local movement. If you believe in community, come join ours.
  
  
**About The Role**
Are you passionate about using data and AI to shape the future of Faire's Go-to-Market (GTM) strategy, sales, and operations? If so, we want to talk to you! We're looking for a technical owner in our team to work closely with Revenue Operations, GTM Systems Engineers, Data Scientists, Product Analysts and Software Engineers to support revenue-driving GTM initiatives by designing and building scalable data capabilities that inform and drive insights. You will lead the path in using AI to boost productivity and transform how analytical engineering operates—leveraging AI to streamline data pipeline development and operations, improve GTM data consumption workflows (e.g., Snowflake, BI tools, Salesforce), and automate routine analytical tasks. In this role, you'll see a direct link between your work, company growth, and user satisfaction. You'll work with A tier players, work with one of the richest data sets in the world, use cutting-edge technology, and see your efforts affect products and SMBs regularly.
  
  
Join us and be part of a team that values data-driven innovation and excellence.
  
  
**What You Will Do**
The ideal candidate will possess a perfect blend of data engineering technical expertise and the product sense to be able to translate internal customer needs into practical solutions. They will have strong skills in developing and supporting large-scale, high-performance data storage and processing systems. They should have a proven track record of collaborating with key stakeholders to execute on GTM data initiatives.
  
  

- Lead cross-team and/or multi-phase projects from design through implementation, fostering collaboration with cross-functional partners.
- Lead the technical execution in building scalable data models and pipelines for a GTM domain defining the architecture, data requirements, and roadmap (e.g., Contact Scoring, Lead Qualification, Sales Pipeline, Sales Enablement).
- Build data pipelines with rigorous approaches to data quality, validation, testing, and observability.
- Redefine how we build, maintain, and scale data systems by embedding AI into our daily workflows.
- Optimize processes for operational excellence in project management and system reliability.
- Ensure security standards are met within data systems and workflows.
- Ensure and promote data quality standards for accurate and reliable insights.
- Embrace emerging technologies with enthusiasm.

**Minimum Qualifications**

- 4+ years experience in analytics & data engineering roles focused on data modeling, large-scale data processing, and tool development for analytics or data science use cases.
- Demonstrated communication and leadership skills, with a history of initiating and steering successful projects across multiple stakeholders.
- Production-level development experience in Python.
- Strong SQL skills with demonstrable competencies in designing well-architected data models and optimizing query performance.
- Deep experience and knowledge of data warehousing concepts, ETLs, big data technologies, and analytics platforms.
- Experience with Airflow, Docker, DBT or similar analytics workflow tools.
- BA/BS degree in Computer Science, Math, Physics, or a related technical field.

**Nice-to-haves**

- Experience with GTM specific data modeling using a BI tool such as Looker or similar while leveraging conversational analytics. Build and own the GTM semantic layer.
- Hands-on experience with GTM-specific data and platform apps, including Reverse ETL (e.g., Hightouch), ELT/Ingestion tools (e.g., Fivetran), iPaaS/Integration platforms (e.g., Workato, N8N, Zapier), and CRM/GTM systems and tool (e.g., Salesforce, Clay).

**Salary Range**
San Francisco: the pay range for this role is $196,000 to $269,500 per year.

data.job_title: Principal AI Engineer, Growth / VP of AI Growth
data.description:
Principal AI Engineer, Growth / VP of AI Growth

**Location:**
San Jose, CA

**Company:**
Typhur

**Compensation:**
$300K–$600K base

**Reporting to:**
Founders

About Typhur

Typhur is a fast-growing smart kitchen appliance brand redefining home cooking through AI-powered hardware, software, and content-driven consumer experiences.

Our products have gained strong traction across YouTube, TikTok, and other social platforms, and we are scaling aggressively in the U.S. market through both retail and direct-to-consumer channels. We are building the next generation of intelligent kitchen products and growth systems that combine consumer hardware, AI, performance marketing, creator content, and full-funnel attribution.

Learn more at
**www.typhur.com**
.

About the Role

Typhur is hiring a
**Principal AI Engineer, Growth / VP of AI Growth**
to lead the AI and algorithmic systems behind our next stage of consumer growth.

This is a rare opportunity for someone with deep experience in recommendation systems, ads optimization, ranking algorithms, content distribution, or growth modeling to apply that expertise directly to a fast-scaling consumer brand.

You will own the AI-driven growth engine across organic content, paid media, DTC traffic, attribution, and conversion optimization. The ideal candidate has worked on algorithmic growth, ads delivery, recommendation, exposure, or performance optimization systems at companies such as Google, YouTube, TikTok, Meta, Amazon, or similar high-scale platforms.

This is a high-ownership, high-impact leadership role reporting directly to the founders. You will have a real seat at the table in shaping how Typhur grows across the U.S. market.

What You’ll Own

AI-Driven Growth Strategy

Build and lead Typhur’s AI-powered growth strategy across paid media, organic content, DTC traffic, creator campaigns, and retail expansion.

Translate platform-level growth insights into brand-level execution, including how content gets discovered, how ads scale, and how budget should be allocated across channels.

Content Performance & Distribution

Develop systems to analyze and improve short-form and long-form video performance across YouTube, YouTube Shorts, TikTok, Instagram, and other platforms.

Identify what drives impressions, engagement, watch time, conversion, and viral lift.

Partner with creative, content, and marketing teams to turn algorithmic insights into repeatable content strategies.

Paid Media & Google Ads Optimization

Own optimization across Google Ads and other performance marketing channels, including impression share, ROAS, bidding strategy, audience targeting, attribution, and budget allocation.

Build AI-driven models to improve media efficiency, reduce wasted spend, and scale profitable acquisition.

DTC Traffic & Conversion Modeling

Develop traffic, conversion, and LTV models to optimize customer acquisition across Typhur’s direct-to-consumer business.

Build systems for full-funnel attribution, cohort analysis, CAC/LTV forecasting, and channel-level ROI measurement.

Use data and AI to improve the entire customer journey from first impression to purchase and repeat engagement.

Growth Infrastructure

Build the data, modeling, and experimentation infrastructure needed to support high-velocity growth.

Design dashboards, models, and decision systems that help the company understand which content, channels, products, and campaigns are driving the most value.

Partner closely with engineering, marketing, product, creative, and founder teams.

What We’re Looking For

Required Qualifications

8+ years of experience in AI, machine learning, data science, recommendation systems, ads optimization, growth algorithms, ranking systems, or performance marketing technology.

Strong hands-on experience with at least one of the following areas:

Recommendation systems

Ads ranking or ads delivery optimization

YouTube / TikTok / social content distribution algorithms

Performance marketing optimization

Growth modeling and attribution

LTV, CAC, ROAS, or conversion modeling

Large-scale experimentation and A/B testing

Deep understanding of how digital platforms optimize exposure, impressions, engagement, and conversion.

Strong technical background in machine learning, statistics, data modeling, and experimentation.

Ability to turn ambiguous business problems into structured models, systems, and measurable outcomes.

Experience working cross-functionally with marketing, product, engineering, analytics, and executive teams.

Strong ownership mindset and ability to operate in a fast-moving, founder-led environment.

Preferred Background

Experience at Google, YouTube, TikTok, Meta, Amazon, Netflix, Snap, Pinterest, or another company with large-scale ads, recommendation, content ranking, or growth systems.

Experience scaling consumer products, DTC brands, marketplace businesses, or subscription / e-commerce products.

Experience with Google Ads, YouTube Ads, TikTok Ads, Meta Ads, or retail media networks.

Experience building internal AI tools for marketing, content analysis, attribution, or budget optimization.

Experience working with consumer hardware, smart devices, IoT, or AI-powered products is a plus but not required.

Why Join Typhur

You will work directly with the founders on one of the company’s most important growth initiatives.

You will have the opportunity to apply platform-level AI and algorithmic thinking to a real consumer brand with strong product-market traction.

You will influence how Typhur scales across content, paid media, DTC, and retail in the U.S.

You will join a fast-growing company at the intersection of AI, consumer hardware, smart kitchens, content, and performance marketing.

You will have high ownership, significant impact, and a clear path to shaping the company’s long-term growth engine.

Compensation

Total compensation for this role ranges from
**$300K to $600K**
, depending on experience, seniority, and scope of responsibility.

Location

This role is based in
**San Jose, CA**
. We are open to candidates who can work closely with the founding team in person or in a hybrid capacity.

Apply

data.description:
### Who you are
- A systems thinker. You see one-off work and want to make it repeatable. You spot patterns and bottlenecks before they become someone's full-time problem
- A builder. You've shipped automations, internal tools, and workflows that made a team measurably faster. You'd rather build than brief
- Comfortable with plumbing. APIs, data flows, enrichment, routing. You know how to connect systems and make them work, even when the docs are wrong
- AI-curious, AI-fluent. You're already experimenting with agents and AI workflows in your own work. You know which abstractions hold up and which are demo-ware
- Bias to ship. You'd rather ship something at 80% this week and improve it next than wait for the perfect spec. There's no playbook for this role and you'll write a lot of it
- A cross-functional translator. You can explain what you're building and why it matters to a CMO, a CRO, and an engineer in the same week. None of them feel talked down to

### What the job involves
- Most GTM engineers wire up someone else's stack
- You'll build a new one. As Mutiny's GTM engineer, you'll own the systems that power how we go to market, from outbound and onboarding to enrichment, routing, and the AI-native workflows that let a small team operate like a much bigger one
- You'll define what GTM engineering looks like at an AI-native company from the ground up
- The GTM stack. The systems behind outbound, onboarding, enrichment, routing, and segmentation. If a workflow is slow or a number is wrong, you fix it at the source
- Automations and internal tools. Build the workflows and tools that make sales and marketing faster. The tool you ship today saves the team a week next month
- The plumbing. APIs, data flows, integrations between every tool we run on. You make systems talk so the team doesn't have to
- AI inside the walls. Bring agents and AI workflows into how we operate, not just what we ship. The way we run becomes the playbook for how customers should run
- Pattern to playbook. Take what's working in one motion and turn it into something repeatable. The automations and tools you build become the foundation the next ten teammates inherit

### Benefits
- You will create a name for yourself by bringing a level of design polish never before seen in this category while developing a spicy brand (in an otherwise boring space).
- You will get exposure to real business problems every company faces (growth) that you can take with you to start your own company (or to help scale another).
- You will have fun, plain and simple. There is a reason our first company value is that work should feel like play.
- You will experience a new way of working. Our team is fully distributed across the US and EU. But we come together as a company for quarterly offsites (usually in super fun places). This combination of experience-based working is a competitive advantage we plan on leaning into.

data.description:
At Thoughtly we're looking for a founding Product Manager to join our team.
  
  
**About Thoughtly**
Thoughtly is the AI-native voice platform that's turning every lead into a real conversation. Thoughtly's agents call, qualify, and convert pipeline at scale — no SDRs, no static funnels, no code required. For the first time in our customers' history, they can reach 100% of all leads. Enterprise teams at 2U, Siemens, and Nomad are augmenting their outbound teams with Thoughtly, with customers seeing close rates jump from 16% to 38% on their lowest-rated leads.
  
  
This is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to build for a $1T annual market. Voice was just the beginning — Thoughtly is building the first fully autonomous CRM. It doesn't just track work — it does the work. The system of record becomes the system of action. This seed-stage rocket ship is backed by top investors like Armory Square Ventures, Afore, and Greycroft.
  
  
**About The Role**
We're looking for a Founding Product Manager to own how Thoughtly shows up in the market — the positioning, the narrative, the competitive edge that turns prospects into believers.
  
  
This role will be the connective tissue between customers, engineering, design, sales, and leadership. You'll be the voice of the customer internally, a key partner to our engineering team as we prioritize and ship, and the person who translates Thoughtly's vision into the product decisions that define the category. You'll work directly with the founding team on hard, unsolved AI product problems: UX for building autonomous agents, real-time conversation intelligence, and the trust required to let AI take action on behalf of revenue teams.
  
  
This is a highly impactful, high-ownership role with real influence over our product roadmap, platform strategy, and long-term direction. If you've been a founder or aspire to be one, we especially want to talk to you.
  
  
**What You'll Do**
**Live in your customers' world.**
Customer empathy is the foundation of everything we build. The people using Thoughtly are sales reps, RevOps leaders, and growth teams trying to convert inbound leads before the window closes — and the end consumers on the other side of those calls have real frustrations, high stakes decisions, and zero patience for a bad experience.
  
  
You'll talk to customers constantly — not to gather requirements, but because you genuinely want to understand how they think, where they feel friction, and what a win looks like to them. You'll ride along on sales calls. You'll listen to call recordings. You'll be the person in the room who can immediately translate a customer's complaint into a crisp product insight. That deep empathy — combined with a clear view of what our platform can and can't do and where the competitive landscape is moving — is how you'll figure out where to invest.
  
  
When you're close enough to customers, the right problems find you.
  
  
**Multiply the team.**
Remove blockers before they become blockers. Nail launch plans so what ships actually gets adopted. De-risk decisions early. Keep everyone aligned without bureaucracy. The team should be accelerated by you, not waiting on you. Most importantly:
**Use AI to build prototypes.**
**Keep pace with AI-native development.**
Conversation AI is moving weekly. Designs still take time. Customer feedback loops still take time. Your job is to compress every non-engineering cycle so we have extraordinary velocity end-to-end — not just in the codebase. Expect to reinvent how you work.
  
  
**Ship product end-to-end.**
Spec it, build it with engineering, test it with customers, launch it, measure it, iterate. Own the metrics — conversation completion rates, lead conversion lift, agent utilization, expansion revenue. Think about what makes someone upgrade, not just what makes someone sign up. Write a PRD when it's useful; skip it and ship a prototype when it's not.
  
  
**Set the quality bar.**
Every conversation our agents have is a first impression for our customers' brands. You'll QA flows personally — not because no one else will, but because that's how you catch what needs to be better. Push for the polish that makes Thoughtly feel like it was built by people who care. Low-ego. If something needs doing, it's yours.
  
  
**Go broad and deep.**
Thoughtly serves multiple high-consideration consumer verticals — real estate, insurance, legal, mortgage, education, and more. Develop real understanding across buyer types and use cases while going deep enough in each to build things that actually solve specific problems.
  
  
**You'll Thrive Here If You**

- 5+ years as a PM, engineer, or founder. You've taken a product from "we think there's something here" to "customers pay for this and renew." Comfortable with ambiguity, structured without a playbook.
- Excellent taste: you notice the details — the copy, the flow, the naming, the speed. You can point to products you admire and articulate specifically why they're great. You want to build products people love, not just products that function.
- Obsessed with the customer. You can't build what you don't feel. You proactively seek out customer conversations — not because someone asked you to, but because you genuinely want to understand how they think. You listen to call recordings on your own time. You feel the friction before the data shows it. And you carry that empathy into every product decision, from naming to pricing to flow.
- Power user of AI - using tools daily and understand the ecosystem
- Metrics-driven. You define success quantitatively. You know which feature-level signals matter and how they ladder to ARR. You back convictions with data and customer insight.
- Maniacal about outcomes. You don't wait to be told what to do. You find what matters, execute, and hold yourself accountable. Process is a tool, not a crutch.
- Willingness to do whatever the team needs: support, testing, ticket cleanup, customer calls.

**Nice to Have**

- B2B / enterprise experience, ideally selling to sales, revenue, or GTM buyers.
- You've built or scaled a horizontal platform across multiple verticals
- Experience at world-class enterprise product orgs (e.g., Gong, Ramp, Stripe, Outreach).

**Compensation**
Thoughtly's compensation package includes a competitive base salary benchmarked against real-time market data, as well as equity for all full-time roles. We also offer exceptional benefits. Our compensation range for this role is: $150,000 – $190,000. Final offers may vary from the amount listed based on geography, candidate experience and expertise, and other factors.
  
  
**Apply for the job**
Do you want to join our team as our new Founding Product Manager? Then we'd love to hear about you!

**About QuadSci**

QuadSci is a breakthrough in AI for customer intelligence that uniquely connects product usage and customer behavior to revenue outcomes. By analyzing trillions of telemetry events, QuadSci helps companies eliminate surprise churn, uncover growth, and align GTM teams on the actions that matter for the business through guidance based on evidence.

QuadSci was recognized as “Machine Learning Company of the Year” in the 8th annual AI Breakthrough Awards program conducted by AI Breakthrough, a leading market intelligence organization that recognizes the top companies, technologies and products in the global Artificial Intelligence (AI) market. Learn more at quadsci.ai.

As we scale, we are investing in the systems that power modern go-to-market. This role sits at the center of that effort.

**About the Role**

This is not a traditional marketing operations role.

As a Marketing GTM Engineer, you will design and build the systems that power how we acquire, activate, and grow customers. You will work across Sales, Marketing and Product to identify inefficiencies, automate workflows, and apply AI to real GTM problems.

You will own projects end-to-end, from initial concept through implementation and adoption, and play a key role in defining how modern GTM teams operate.

**Responsibilities**

- Design and build AI-powered workflows that scale acquisition, activation, and pipeline
- Build and maintain integrations across GTM systems, ensuring clean, reliable data flow
- Design and run experiments across outbound, routing, enrichment, and other GTM motions, and use results to inform what to scale
- Develop and refine AI-powered workflows, including prompts, agents, and models, to improve execution and decision-making
- Own projects end-to-end, from prototyping through rollout and enablements
- Track performance, adoption, and ROI of systems and experiments, and iterate based on results
- Continuously evaluate and experiment with emerging tools and approaches in AI and GTM
- Act as an internal subject-matter expert on AI-driven GTM systems

**Qualifications**

- 3–5 years of experience in GTM Engineering, Marketing Operations or a similar technical role
- Proficiency in Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, SQL, or similar for building custom logic and integrations
- Hands-on experience with GTM systems such as Salesforce or similar platforms at a system or integration level
- Practical experience applying AI or LLMs within workflows (e.g., OpenAI/Claude APIs or similar)
- Strong ability to design experiments, analyze results, and measure impact
- Proven ability to take projects from 0→1, from experimentation through adoption
- Degree in decision intelligence, marketing analytics, computer science or similar field

**Location**

- Remote-first, with occasional in-person collaboration.

**Note**

GTM Engineering is an emerging discipline. We don’t expect candidates to check every box. We’re looking for curious builders who are excited to define how modern GTM systems are designed, automated, and scaled.

data.description:
**We’re Cubby Beds 👋**
A fast-growing and high-impact startup that makes smart beds for people with cognitive conditions like Epilepsy, Cerebral Palsy, Dementia and Autism. Our mission is to improve the lives of special needs kids and their families through our innovative products and go to market strategies. Since launch, we have changed the lives of thousands of families, been insurance covered in all 50 states, achieved profitability, and are building a growing team in our Denver HQ.
  
  
The next stage of the business is all about scaling our team, product, systems, and customer journey to help thousands more families get the safe sleep they deserve.
  
  
**This is where you come in**
When you join Cubby, you’re joining a team of builders and problem-solvers working to create meaningful change for families who need it most.
  
  
As our Senior Product Engineer (AI & Growth Systems), you will design, build, and deploy AI-powered workflows, growth infrastructure, and internal tools that directly improve how Cubby operates and scales.
  
  
This role sits at the intersection of engineering, product thinking, and go-to-market systems. You will build both internal operational tools and customer-facing growth infrastructure—from automating workflows for Sales and CX teams to launching new website pages, marketing systems, and data-driven experiences that help families discover and access Cubby.
  
  
Your success will be measured not by experimentation alone, but by real adoption, measurable efficiency gains, and tangible business outcomes.
  
  
You will work closely with leaders across Sales, Marketing, CX, Product, and Operations to identify high-leverage opportunities, ship systems teams rely on daily, and continuously improve them based on real-world usage.
  
  
This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building practical systems that make a company run better—combining AI tools, APIs, business systems, and web infrastructure to deliver meaningful impact.
  
  
**Here’s What You’ll Be Doing**

- Shipping High-Impact AI Workflows – Design, build, and deploy AI-powered workflows, customer experiences or internal tools that are actively used by business teams and tied to measurable operational or revenue impact.
- Delivering Measurable Efficiency Gains – Reduce manual work, turnaround time, or error rates in targeted workflows, resulting in documented time savings or cost reduction.
- Establishing Practical AI Standards & Guardrails – Implement monitoring, documentation, fallback logic, and quality controls to ensure AI systems are reliable and trustworthy.
- Driving Adoption Across Teams – Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to ensure tools are adopted, understood, and continuously improved based on real feedback.
- Providing Leadership Visibility into Impact – Report clear metrics on usage, performance, and ROI of AI systems deployed.
- Help define what this role looks like at Cubby—there's no playbook yet

**Here’s What We’re Looking For**

- 4+ years experience building and shipping production systems, automation, or internal tools
- Ability to integrate APIs, data systems, and modern AI tooling
- Experience applying LLM-powered workflows or AI systems in real-world environments
- Comfort building with AI-assisted development tools (Claude, Copilot, Cursor, etc.)
- Strong product instincts and the ability to connect technical work to business outcomes
- Comfort operating in ambiguous environments without detailed specs
- A bias toward shipping solutions and iterating quickly

**Ideally you also have…**

- A passion for exploring and implementing modern AI approaches such as prompt engineering, agents, or automation workflows
- Background in highly regulated spaces like healthcare and financial services
- Experience building product in early-stage startups or high-ambiguity environments

**To Apply:**
All applications must be submitted through the Job Openings section of the Cubby Beds Careers site
  
  
**Don’t feel like you have all of the qualifications?**
The description above indicates our current vision for the role. You could be a viable candidate even if you don't fit everything we've described above and may also have important skills we haven't thought of. If that's you - even if you’re unsure - we encourage you to apply and help us get to know you!
  
  
**Benefits & Perks**
✨
  
  
- Compensation: At Cubby, we reward great performance with clear, motivating compensation. We believe in aligning incentives and sharing wins.
- Total Comp for this Role: $150,000-180,000
- This includes:

- Base Salary: $136,000-164,000
- Performance Bonus: Up to 10% of your base salary if targets are hit
- Stock Options - Equity Ownership
- Health, Dental, and Vision Insurance
- Unlimited PTO & Sick/Wellness Hours
- 12 paid holidays, a paid Volunteer Day and a Powder Day
- 401k with a company match
- Hybrid Work Model: Monday-Wednesday in the office, optional work from home Thursday-Friday
- Mac, standing desk, and high-end accessories at the office + a work-from-home stipend to ﻿purchase equipment needed for home offices
- Paid parking
- Annual staff retreat
- Stocked kitchen and bimonthly team lunches in our beautiful Denver HQ
- Growth opportunities at a start-up with a life-changing mission

If you have the desire to build meaningful AI-driven products and workflows in a high-growth org that also changes lives, we want to meet you! Interested? Apply now! 👩🏻‍💻
  
  
Cubby Beds is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. Cubby Beds is also committed to compliance with all fair employment practices regarding citizenship and immigration status. For data removal requests from our Applicant Tracking System, please email recruiting@cubbybeds.com.
  
  
This position will remain open until filled.
  
  
Compensation Range: $150K - $180K

**Location:**
Remote (Americas time zones preferred)

**Type:**
Full-time

**Reports to:**
CEO / Head of Growth\

**Why this role exists**

Finta is building the Capital Copilot—an AI workspace where deals run themselves. This role exists to make

the product grow itself by shipping experiments and improvements that increase activation, retention,

conversion, and PQL volume/quality—and by building the instrumentation that powers routing into the

right queue.

**Captain / Co-pilot ownership**

**Captain (primary):**
Visitor → Signup (demand + conversion), and the measurement layer that

defines whether accounts qualify for the next queue.

**Co-pilot (supports any stage)**
: Can run education campaigns and product messaging across the

lifecycle.

**Non-negotiable:**
Growth never makes 1:1 onboarding or commercial commitments; the captain for

the queue decides sequence.

**What success looks like**

**30 days**
- Implement/validate the account lifecycle dictionary in analytics: stage, queue, captain, last-touch. -

Instrument the activation funnel end-to-end (signup → connect inbox → create deal → add contacts →

upload/share docs) with QA. - Identify top 3 drop-offs and ship the first 3 experiments.

**90 days**
- Operate a weekly growth cadence: hypotheses → ship → measure → decide. - Improve at least

one of: activation rate, time-to-activation, self-serve conversion. - Define initial PQL triggers (Expansion-

Ready signals) with CS/Sales alignment and route cleanly into the Expansion Queue.

**6 months -**
Own a compounding roadmap across onboarding, lifecycle nudges, referrals, and conversion

surfaces that supports $1M ARR by EOY 2026. - Increase PQL quality (not just volume) by reducing false

positives and improving routing clarity.

**What you’ll do**

Ship product changes that move funnel metrics (onboarding, activation, upgrade, referrals,

performance).

Build and run A/B tests and holdouts with clean measurement.

Define event schemas and maintain analytics integrity (stage/queue/captain accuracy).

Build dashboards for:

Activation funnel and time-to-value

PQL triggers (Expansion-Ready signals)

Self-serve conversion and expansion assists

Partner with product/CS to create in-app nudges and lifecycle messaging that accelerate activation.

Build experimentation and landing-page infrastructure to move faster.

**Must-haves**

- Strong shipping ability (front-end + back-end fundamentals).
- Comfortable with data and experimentation (cohorts, attribution, event integrity).
- Can translate ambiguous goals into measurable tests.
- Speed with rigor: fast iterations without analytics chaos.

**Nice-to-haves**

- PLG/self-serve SaaS experience.
- Experience with PostHog/Mixpanel/GA4 and lifecycle systems.
- Experience building PQL scoring/triggers and routing.

****About Us****
At LangChain, our mission is to make intelligent agents ubiquitous. We build the foundation for agent engineering in the real world, helping developers move from prototypes to production-ready AI agents that teams can rely on. We began as widely adopted open-source tools and have grown to also offer a platform for building, evaluating, deploying, and operating agents at scale.
  
  
With $125M raised at Series B from IVP, Sequoia, Benchmark, CapitalG, and Sapphire Ventures, we’re at a stage where we’re continuing to develop new products, growth is accelerating, and all team members have meaningful impact on what we build and how we work together. LangChain is a place where your contributions can shape how this technology shows up in the real world.
  
  
Today, LangChain, LangGraph, LangSmith, and Fleet are used by teams shipping real AI products across startups and large enterprises. Millions of developers trust LangChain to power AI teams at companies like Replit, Clay, Coinbase, Workday, Lyft, Cloudflare, Harvey, Rippling, Vanta, and 35% of the Fortune 500.
  
  
**About the Role:**
We are looking for an
**GTM Engineer**
where you won’t just be using our tools—you’ll be the "First Customer," building the AI-native systems that power our technical support, onboarding, and customer success engines.
  
  
You will own the technical roadmap for how LangChain supports its users. Your goal is to drive massive
**case deflection**
and a premium
**onboarding experience**
by building autonomous agents that solve complex technical problems before a human ever needs to step in. This is a role for a
**builder-operator**
who can identify a friction point in the customer journey and ship a production-grade agentic solution to fix it.
  
  
****You Will:****

- Architect Customer Agents: Design and deploy production-grade agents (using LangGraph and LangSmith) that handle technical support queries, troubleshoot integrations, and guide users through complex onboarding flows.
- Drive Case Deflection: Analyze customer friction points and build self-service AI systems that significantly reduce support volume while improving the quality of the customer experience.
- Own the Domain: Act as the product owner and the technical muscle proactively identifying opportunities for improvement, propose architectures, and own the full lifecycle of the systems you build.
- Dogfood the Stack: Be a key member of the feedback loop for our product team. As you build complex systems for our customers, you’ll identify gaps in our frameworks and contribute back to the LangChain and LangGraph open-source ecosystem.
- Build Onboarding Workflows: Develop "AI-native onboarding" experiences that help enterprise customers move from prototypes to production faster by automating documentation retrieval and code generation.

**What We Are Looking For:**

- AI-Native Developer: You have a deep understanding of the LLM stack: prompting, retrieval (RAG), cognitive architectures, and agentic loops. You have likely already built with LangChain or LangGraph.
- Engineering Foundation: You are a strong software engineer (typically 3+ years) with at least 1 year of experience specifically shipping LLM systems in production.
- Self-Directed "Founder" Mentality: You don't need a ticket to tell you what to fix. You are comfortable navigating ambiguity, identifying high-impact problems, and driving them to completion autonomously.
- Full-Stack Capability: Strong coding skills in Python or TypeScript (ideally both) and the ability to build end-to-end applications.
- Customer-Centric: You enjoy the intersection of high-level engineering and direct customer impact. You can translate a customer's technical pain point into a scalable system architecture.

****Nice To Haves:****

- Expertise with LangSmith for evaluation and monitoring.
- Experience building or maintaining open-source projects.
- A background in technical consulting, solutions engineering, or high-growth GTM teams.

****Compensation**
:**

- We offer competitive compensation that includes base salary, meaningful equity, benefits, and perks. Benefits include things like medical, dental, and vision coverage, flexible vacation, a 401(k) plan, and life insurance.
- Salary: $160K - $180K

company_object.long_description: Sequoia Capital Global Equities ("SCGE") is a public/private crossover firm that was founded in 2009 as an affiliate of Sequoia Capital. SCGE extends Sequoia Capital's technology investing into the public markets and applies the firm's thematic investment approach across the internet, software, semiconductor and fintech sectors. We are a long-term partner to transformational and enduring technology companies through their journey to IPO and beyond.
